question: Tell us about yourself — your educational background, the stream you studied, and what motivated you to pursue a career in software development.
Why are you interested in this role? What do you know about our company and what excites you about joining us as a fresher?
Explain the difference between Java and Python. In what scenarios would you prefer one over the other?
What are the four pillars of Object-Oriented Programming? Explain each one with a simple real-world example.
Describe your final year or most significant academic project. What was the problem you solved, what technologies did you use, and what was your role in the team?
How do you approach finding and fixing a bug in your code? Walk us through your debugging process with an example from your studies or projects.
Have you used any framework such as Spring Boot, Django, or React — either in college projects or self-learning? What did you build and what challenges did you face?
Describe a situation where you had a disagreement with a classmate or group member during a college project. How did you handle it and what was the result?
What new technology, tool, or concept have you learned recently on your own outside of your college curriculum? How did you learn it and how do you plan to use it?
Where do you see yourself professionally in the next three to five years, and how does this role help you get there?
